html:
Exclusive to:

The Ancient Orchestrina is a naturally spawning object exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them. It can be found in the Ancient Archive biome.

The Ancient Orchestrina is inactive until activated by the Archive Switch. To start a puzzle, a player must drop a Distilled Knowledge on the circle. The puzzle requires stepping on the eight outer circles in the correct numerical order. Each circle flashes a number when stepped on. The order is randomized and the correct sequence can only be found through trial and error.

Making a mistake causes the puzzle to revert 1 step. However, if more than half of the sequence has been completed (5 or more), an error will instead revert progress by 2 steps.

Once solved, the Distilled Knowledge is consumed, and all nearby players get one Blueprint. Players who have already unlocked the item will not receive new Blueprints.

Trivia

  • The Ancient Orchestrina was introduced in the Forgotten Knowledge update.
  • The circles on the outer edge could represent phases of the moon.

Gallery

  • The Ancient Orchestrina in-game, with a Distilled Knowledge placed in its center.
DSTDSRoGSWHam
plainText:
Ancient Orchestrina
Object Info
Perk
Unlocks Blueprints.
Source
Ancient Archive
Miscellaneous
"archive_orchestrina_base"
Exclusive to:
“It's like they made it puzzling on purpose.”
–Wilson
“It won't burn? Well, I'm out of ideas.”
–Willow
“Floor is making sounds!”
–Wolfgang
“Abigail was better at these things.”
–Wendy
“THIS DEVICE OUTLIVED ITS CREATORS. AN INSPIRATION”
–WX-78
“There must be a manual somewhere.”
–Wickerbottom
“That's one fancy floor, eh?”
–Woodie
“Rather devious.”
–Maxwell
“What if I just stab it?”
–Wigfrid
“We love puzzles!”
–Webber
“It's a mystery to me.”
–Warly
“Singing floor”
–Wormwood
“Some mighty complicated engineering went into this.”
–Winona
“This floor is soundly built, hyuyu!”
–Wortox
“Why floor make weird sounds, florp?”
–Wurt
“Wanna solve a puzzle, Woby?”
–Walter
“What an intricate design.”
–Wanda
The Ancient Orchestrina is a naturally spawning object exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them. It can be found in the Ancient Archive biome.
The Ancient Orchestrina is inactive until activated by the Archive Switch. To start a puzzle, a player must drop a Distilled Knowledge on the circle.
The puzzle requires stepping on the eight outer circles in the correct numerical order. Each circle flashes a number when stepped on. The order is randomized and the correct sequence can only be found through trial and error.
Making a mistake causes the puzzle to revert 1 step. However, if more than half of the sequence has been completed (5 or more), an error will instead revert progress by 2 steps.
Once solved, the Distilled Knowledge is consumed, and all nearby players get one Blueprint. Players who have already unlocked the item will not receive new Blueprints.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Ancient Orchestrina was introduced in the Forgotten Knowledge update.
The circles on the outer edge could represent phases of the moon.
Gallery[edit | edit source]
The Ancient Orchestrina in-game, with a Distilled Knowledge placed in its center.

The '''Ancient Orchestrina''' is a naturally spawning object exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in ''[[Return of Them]]''. It can be found in the [[Ancient Archive]] biome.

The Ancient Orchestrina is inactive until activated by the [[Archive Switch]]. To start a puzzle, a player must drop a [[Distilled Knowledge]] on the circle.  
The puzzle requires stepping on the eight outer circles in the correct numerical order. Each circle flashes a number when stepped on. The order is randomized and the correct sequence can only be found through trial and error.  

Making a mistake causes the puzzle to revert 1 step. However, if more than half of the sequence has been completed (5 or more), an error will instead revert progress by 2 steps.

Once solved, the Distilled Knowledge is consumed, and all nearby players get one Blueprint. Players who have already unlocked the item will not receive new Blueprints.

{{clear}}

* The circles on the outer edge could represent phases of the moon.
